sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard (formerly CAC/RS 28-1970) contains general requirements applicable to all edible fungi, whether fresh or processed, permitted for sale by the competent authorities in the consuming countries, except canned cultivated mushrooms of the genus Agaricus. Different requirements for the products covered by this standard may be laid down in group of products standards or in individual standards.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard (formerly CAC/RS 28-1970) contains general requirements applicable to all edible fungi, whether fresh or processed, permitted for sale by the competent authorities in the consuming countries, except canned cultivated mushrooms of the genus Agaricus. Different requirements for the products covered by this standard may be laid down in group of products standards or in individual standards.

page_spans.text: This standard (formerly CAC/RS 28-1970) contains general requirements applicable to all edible fungi, whether fresh or processed, permitted for sale by the competent authorities in the consuming countries, except canned cultivated mushrooms of the genus Agaricus. Different requirements for the products covered by this standard may be laid down in group of products standards or in individual standards.

sections.title: Quick frozen fungi means fresh edible fungi of one species which, after cleaning, washing and blanching, are subjected to a freezing process in appropriate equipment and comply with the conditions laid down hereafter in this section and in subsection 7.2 of this standard. This freezing operation shall be carried out in such a way that the range of temperature of maximum crystallization is passed quickly. The quick-freezing process shall not be regarded as complete unless and until the product temperature has reached -18 oC (0 oF) at the thermal centre after thermal stabilization.

sections.title: Examination and sorting of raw material
path_titles: Examination and sorting of raw material
sections.text: As there are edible fungi which closely resemble inedible or poisonous fungi, care shall be taken to ensure, when the fungi are being picked, that only those of the same edible species are collected.  Where such care has not been adequately exercized, the edible fungi species shall be sorted from the collected fungi, before they are marketed, preserved or used in the preparation of fungus products.  Wild fungi which are to be marketed or preserved, or used in the preparation of fungus products shall be carefully examined by an expert to determine whether there are any inedible fungi amongst them, and such inedible fungi shall be removed.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Examination and sorting of raw material
number=2.4
level=1
As there are edible fungi which closely resemble inedible or poisonous fungi, care shall be taken to ensure, when the fungi are being picked, that only those of the same edible species are collected.  Where such care has not been adequately exercized, the edible fungi species shall be sorted from the collected fungi, before they are marketed, preserved or used in the preparation of fungus products.  Wild fungi which are to be marketed or preserved, or used in the preparation of fungus products shall be carefully examined by an expert to determine whether there are any inedible fungi amongst them, and such inedible fungi shall be removed.

page_spans.text: As there are edible fungi which closely resemble inedible or poisonous fungi, care shall be taken to ensure, when the fungi are being picked, that only those of the same edible species are collected.  Where such care has not been adequately exercized, the edible fungi species shall be sorted from the collected fungi, before they are marketed, preserved or used in the preparation of fungus products.  Wild fungi which are to be marketed or preserved, or used in the preparation of fungus products shall be carefully examined by an expert to determine whether there are any inedible fungi amongst them, and such inedible fungi shall be removed.

sections.title: Fungus products – general requirements
sections.text:
Raw material: only fresh edible fungi which have been treated or processed immediately after they have been picked, before deterioration sets in, may be used in the preparation of fungus products.  Both as raw material and as preserved fungi, they shall be healthy, clean, undamaged, free, as far as possible, of maggot damage and possess the flavour and taste appropriate to the species.

Fungus products may contain salt (sodium chloride), vinegar, spices and herbs, sugars (any carbohydrate sweetening matter), refined edible vegetable oil, refined edible animal fat, butter, milk, milk powder, cream, water and wine.

sections.title: Other styles
sections.text:
Any other presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that the product:
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in this standard;
b) meets all relevant requirements of this standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements in this standard which are applicable to that style in the standard which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),1 and other codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product.
5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
- shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health;
- shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and
- shall not contain any substance originating from microorganisms in amounts which may represent  a hazard to health.
5.4 Products covered by this standard which are in the dried or dehydrated form should be prepared in accordance with the provisions of the Code of Hygienic Practice for Dehydrated Fruits and Vegetables including Edible Fungi (CXC 5-1971)2 recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
5.5 Products covered by this standard which have been pasteurized in hermetically -sealed containers should be prepared in accordance with the Code of Hygienic Practice for Canned Fruit and Vegetable Products (CXC 2-1969)3 recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
5.6 Products covered by this standard which are in the quick-frozen state should be prepared in accordance with the Code of Hygienic Practice for the Processing and Handling of Quick-Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).4
5.7 Products covered by this standard which do not fall within one of the categories at 5.4, 5.5 and 5.6 above, for example, fresh edible fungi, should be prepared in accordance with the relevant sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969).1

sections.title: WEIGHTS AND MEASURES
sections.text:
6.1 Fill of container
Minimum fill: The container shall be well filled with mushrooms and the product (including packing medium) shall occupy not less than 90 percent of the water capacity of the container.  The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20 oC which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

sections.title: Tolerances for defects
sections.text:
a) Mineral impurities not more than 0.3% m/m 
b) Organic impurities of vegetable origin not more than 0.05% m/m

sections.title: PACKING, STORAGE AND TRANSPORTATION
sections.text: The packaging used for fresh fungi shall be perforated to allow the free passage of air, if needed. The product shall be maintained at a low temperature such as will maintain the quality during transportation, storage and distribution up to and including the time of final sale. The recognized practice of thawing and repacking products under controlled conditions followed by the application of the quick -freezing process as defined in subsection 2.1.12 of this standard is permitted. In the case of a) Dried Fungi, and b) Fungus Grits and Fungus Powder, attention is directed to the need to prevent these products from absorbing moisture and being attacked by insects, particularly moths and mites.

sections.title: The name of the food
sections.text: Products complying with the definitions and other requirements of this standard shall be appropriately designated to indicate their true nature. The terms "fungus" and "fungi" may be replaced by the terms which have customarily been used to describe the genus or species concerned in the country in which the product is intended to be sold, e.g. "mushroom" or "mushrooms" for the genus Agaricus. The method of processing to which the product has been subjected, e.g. "dried", "sterilized" or "quick-frozen", shall be indicated on the label.

sentences.original:
Any other presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that the product:
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in this standard;
b) meets all relevant requirements of this standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements in this standard which are applicable to that style in the standard which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

classification.sentence:
Any other presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that the product:
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in this standard;
b) meets all relevant requirements of this standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements in this standard which are applicable to that style in the standard which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.
classification.function: Permit other presentations if conditions a), b), and c) are met
classification.pattern: Any other presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that the product: a) [condition a]; b) [condition b]; c) [condition c].

components.condition a: is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in this standard
components.condition b: meets all relevant requirements of this standard, including limitations on defects, drained weight, and other applicable requirements
components.condition c: is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer

classification.justification: Permission with multiple conditions; partial match to conditional permission templates.

pages.summary: Página inicial com título principal do documento e cabeçalho de identificação, sem seções detalhadas ou texto adicional.
Página contendo dois títulos principais não numerados em caixa alta, cada um com texto explicativo associado, sem continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém dois títulos de nível 1 numerados: '1. SCOPE' com texto explicativo e '2. DESCRIPTION' que atua como agrupador. Dentro de '2. DESCRIPTION' há uma subseção '2.1 Definition of products' e múltiplas subdivisões 3 (2.1.1 a 2.1.18) cada uma com título-texto curto, mas sem texto adicional separado. Não há sinal de continuidade interpaginas.

Página contém a continuação da seção 2 com subseções detalhadas numeradas 2.2 a 2.4, definindo tipos de defeitos e aspectos relacionados a matérias-primas. Em seguida, inicia a seção 3 como título nível 1 em caixa alta com subseções numeradas 3.1 e 3.2 e seus subníveis detalhados. Texto está organizado de forma clara com títulos seguidos por texto explicativo. Marcação de continuidade indica que a seção 2 iniciou em página anterior e a seção 3 continua na próxima página. Não há documento título explícito visível na página.

Página contém subseções detalhadas numeradas 3.2.2 a 3.3.2.1, estruturadas hierarquicamente com títulos e texto explicativo. A seção 3.2 tem continuidade desde página anterior e segue na próxima, com vários subtítulos 3.2.x. A seção 3.3 inicia nesta página com subseções numeradas. O texto está limpo e organizado, sem inclusão de cabeçalhos ou rodapés no conteúdo. Marcações de continuidade entre páginas estão indicadas conforme regras.

Página contém subtítulos detalhados num formato hierárquico 3.3.2.2 a 3.3.5.2, todos exibidos claramente com seus textos associados. Não há indicações de continuidade desde páginas anteriores nem continuação para próximas páginas nesta página. O cabeçalho da página é identificado e excluído do texto interno dos nós para preservar a qualidade para RAG.

A página apresenta uma hierarquia clara começando com seções nível 1 numeradas 3.3.6 a 3.3.10, cada uma contendo subseções nível 2 numeradas terminando em .1, .2 ou .3. Nenhuma seção ou subseção continua da página anterior ou para a próxima, e o texto relacionado está associado corretamente a cada título, preservando a numeração e títulos para facilitar a recuperação e geração de resposta (RAG). O cabeçalho "CXS 38-1981 7" foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós.

A página apresenta hierarquia detalhada das seções 3.3.10.2, 4 (com subtítulos 4.1 a 4.7), 5 e 6, com textos associados corretamente e sem continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho 'CXS 38-1981 8' foi identificado e excluído dos conteúdos dos nós para melhor qualidade no RAG.

A página apresenta continuação da seção 6 com o item 6.2, seguido pelas seções 7, 8 e 9, todas numeradas e com títulos claros. A seção 6.2 continua da página anterior. As subseções da 7 e 8 são identificadas corretamente com seus textos associados ou vazios quando funcionam como agrupadores. Não há continuidade para a próxima página na hierarquia. O cabeçalho da página é identificado e excluído do conteúdo para melhor qualidade no RAG.

A página contém uma única seção principal 'NOTES' em nível 1, seguida por uma lista numerada de notas bibliográficas. Não há subseções detectadas. O conteúdo está completo nesta página sem continuidade para ou da anterior.
